Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We are going to create a DECLARE model for the given scenario We will define constraints that are typical for such a process and assign them support and confidence values Since the scenario is a typical product design and launch process we can assume that the constraints are always followed so support and confidence are 1 0

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - IND
Text: However note in real life constraints might not be followed 100 but for the purpose of this example we assume they are

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We will define constraints for each of the DECLARE template types but note that the scenario might not require every type

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We will consider the following constraints Existence constraints existence At least one occurrence of an activity in each trace Absence constraints absence An activity does not occur in the trace Exactly one constraints exactly one An activity occurs exactly once in the trace Init constraints init The trace must start with a given activity

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We also have relationship constraints like responded existence precedence and so on between two activities We will define some constraints that make sense for the process

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: Lets list the activities again for reference IG DD TFC CE PC LT UT AG MP FL

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are to create a dictionary with the given keys and values

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The instructions say that for the first set of keys existence absence exactly one init the value is a dictionary with activities as keys and support confidence as value For the second set of keys responded existence and so on the value is a dictionary with activities as keys and support confidence as value but these constraints are between two activities so the keys are typically a tuple or string representing a pair in DECLARE

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Since the prompt says containing as keys the activities I think for the second set the dictionary keys are the activities or more precisely the pair of activities and the value is the support and confidence

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Looking at the pm4py documentation I see that for DECLARE constraints the keys are often strings of the form A B for a constraint between A and B Lets assume that format

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Since this is a made up scenario we have to make reasonable constraints

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Existence We require that each trace must contain at least one occurrence of Idea Generation IG Design Draft DD Technical Feasibility Check TFC Cost Evaluation CE Prototype Creation PC Laboratory Testing LT User Testing UT Approval Gate AG Marketing Plan MP Final Launch FL

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: But note In a real process not every activity might be required However for the sake of the example lets say all are required at least once

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Exactly one We might require that Approval Gate AG happens exactly once since its a gate Final Launch FL happens exactly once

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Absence We might require that there is no activity after Final Launch FL in the trace But note absence is for the entire trace so we cannot have FL at all if we use absence FL So we do not want that Instead we might have that certain activities are absent in the entire trace Not typical for this process So we might leave absence constraints empty

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Init The process must start with Idea Generation IG

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Now for the binary constraints We will define some typical ones responded existence If Design Draft DD occurs then Technical Feasibility Check TFC must occur precedence Approval Gate AG must occur before Final Launch FL response If Prototype Creation PC occurs then Laboratory Testing LT must occur after PC succession Idea Generation IG must be followed by Design Draft DD altresponse If User Testing UT occurs then Approval Gate AG must occur after UT and there cannot be another UT until AG occurs chainresponse After Cost Evaluation CE Prototype Creation PC must occur immediately

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: noncoexistence Idea Generation IG and Final Launch FL cannot occur in the same trace which makes sense because IG is at the beginning and FL at the end but they both occur so we do not want this constraint Instead we might not have noncoexistence constraints in this process

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We will set support and confidence to 1 0 for all these constraints

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are to create a dictionary with all the keys from the DECLARE model even if we do not have constraints for some of them We can leave them as empty dictionaries

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Create the dictionary with the required keys For each constraint type assign a dictionary of constraints with the constraint as key and a tuple support confidence as value We will use strings for the binary constraints
